title: Participant runtime + supervisor evidence-backed administrative ApplyTransition
summary: Exercises ACP Checkpoint G (participant runtime create/resume + lifecycle) and Checkpoint H (supervisor AttachEvidence/ApplyTransition/Pause/Unpause control actions with capability-and-provenance authorization). The supervisor never claims a workflow role itself; instead, ApplyTransition uses evidenceRefs whose provenance the kernel verifies points back to a role-bound participant run, recording authority='supervisor_from_participant_evidence' on the transition event. Pause/Unpause produce real state changes that gate subsequent control actions.

task.goal: Drive a code-change task end to end via participant runs, with supervisor evidence-backed authority moving the workflow.

steps.summary: Supervisor creates the workflow task pinned to participant_supervisor_demo@1, binds implementer=larry, tester=curly.

args.goal: Drive a code-change task end to end via participant runs, with supervisor evidence-backed authority moving the workflow.

steps.summary: Implementer attaches a plan_record (standalone evidence attach) — required by the start transition.

evidence.summary: Plan: guard nil order id and add a regression test.

steps.summary: User-direct path: launch a participant run for the implementer role via `acp task run --task <id> --role implementer --agent larry`. The kernel verifies actor matches the persisted role binding (no body actor self-claim). Records the run with status='launched'.
Implementer participant run attaches commit_ref and regression_test, supplying its own runId so provenance records participantRunId=<run from step 04>.
Implementer participant run is marked completed with outcome=success, referencing the two evidence records.

Supervisor uses ApplyTransition control action with evidenceRefs pointing at the two implementer-run-provenance evidence records. Kernel verifies (a) evidence belongs to task, (b) evidence satisfies implement_fix evidence requirement, (c) evidence was attached by a participant run (not the supervisor), (d) participant run role 'implementer' is in transition.by, (e) participant run actor matches current binding. transition.applied event payload records authority='supervisor_from_participant_evidence', supervisorRunId=<active>, evidenceRefs=[...].
User-direct path: launch a participant run for the tester role via `acp task run --task <id> --role tester --agent curly`.
Tester participant run attaches verification_report with run provenance.

Supervisor pauses its own supervision run via PauseSupervision control action. Records supervisor.paused event and updates the supervisor run record to paused=true. Subsequent supervisor control actions on this run must reject with code supervisor_paused.

controlAction.reason: Manual reviewer break — pausing automation while reviewer evaluates verification_report.

steps.summary: While paused, supervisor attempts AttachEvidence — must reject with supervisor_paused.
Supervisor unpauses; supervisor run record paused=false; subsequent control actions accepted again.

Tester (role-bound) applies verify directly. SoD requires acting actor != implementer (curly != larry, OK). The role-mode path is exercised here — supervisor authority is NOT used for verify, demonstrating that both authority modes coexist.

negativeChecks.name: user-launch-with-unbound-role-rejected
negativeChecks.summary: On a fresh task with tester role unbound, attempt `acp task run --role tester --agent curly`. Expect rejection code role_not_bound — the user-direct endpoint does NOT bind-and-launch.

negativeChecks.name: user-launch-actor-mismatch-rejected
negativeChecks.summary: Attempt `acp task run --role implementer --agent rex` when implementer is bound to larry. Expect rejection code role_not_bound (or sod_violation) — body actor cannot self-claim.

negativeChecks.name: supervisor-apply-transition-with-supervisor-only-evidence-rejected
negativeChecks.summary: On a fresh task, supervisor attaches all required evidence under supervisor authority (no participant run involved), then attempts ApplyTransition control action with those evidenceRefs. Expect rejection — evidence must be attached by a participant run for ApplyTransition. Rejection code authority_not_granted (or invalid_evidence).

negativeChecks.name: supervisor-apply-transition-without-evidenceRefs-rejected
negativeChecks.summary: Attempt ApplyTransition control action WITHOUT supplying evidenceRefs (i.e., relying on 'all evidence by kind on task' as the role-mode transition path does). Expect rejection — supervisor authority requires explicit evidenceRefs. Rejection code missing_evidence (or evidenceRefs_required).

negativeChecks.name: supervisor-without-applySupervisorTransitions-cap-rejected
negativeChecks.summary: Recreate the task with the supervisor binding lacking applySupervisorTransitions capability. Attempt step 07. Expect rejection code capability_not_granted.

negativeChecks.name: request-body-capability-override-ignored
negativeChecks.summary: Supply a request body that claims supervisor capabilities the persisted supervisor run record does NOT have. Per the cody-mandated authorization hardening, capabilities must derive from the persisted run record, not the request body. Expect rejection code capability_not_granted.

negativeChecks.name: pause-without-pauseSupervision-cap-rejected
negativeChecks.summary: Recreate supervisor binding without pauseSupervision capability. Attempt step 10. Expect rejection code capability_not_granted.
